title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Compare Term Insurance - Your Key to Finding the Ideal Coverage
Having a reliable life insurance policy in place may be crucial if you wish to protect the future of your loved ones financially. Among the various types of life insurance available, term insurance may be an option to consider, as it may offer a higher level of coverage at an affordable premium.
However, due to the numerous options available in the market, finding the right term plan may be a daunting task. To make this task easier, you may want to compare term insurance plans in a smart manner. Comparing the various aspects of different term insurance policies may help you make an informed decision and choose the coverage best fitting your needs and budget.
With that context, let’s understand why it is important to compare term insurance plans and how you may do so.
Importance of Term Plan Comparisons
Here are a few reasons why comparing term plans may prove to be beneficial for you:
Choose better features and benefits
When you compare term plans, you may get an opportunity to understand and go through the different features and benefits of term policies offered by various insurance companies. With a thorough knowledge of what each plan may offer, you may be able to make a better and well-informed decision of choosing the one that meets your needs.
Get clarity on the insurer’s reputation
Choosing the right policy may also involve selecting the right insurance provider. Hence, when you compare a term insurance plan with another, you may also end up comparing the term plan providers as well. You may get a clearer idea of the insurer, the features they offer, their reputation and even their claim settlement record.
This may help you choose the right insurance provider with whom you may have a pleasant experience throughout your term insurance journey.
Save money
A comparison of term plans may help you save money in the long run. Different insurance providers offer different premiums for their policies. Hence, it may be crucial to compare the different premium rates to find a policy fitting your budget. This may increase your chances of getting comprehensive coverage at a reasonable price.
How to Compare Term Insurance Plans?
Now that you may know why term insurance comparison is important, let’s learn about the factors you may take into consideration in the process:
Balance of premium and coverage
The premium and the coverage may be the two most important determinants of what makes a good term plan. However, you should not focus highly on one and ignore the other. When you compare one term policy with another, you may want to see the extent of coverage of each plan. Then, you may go ahead and check the estimated premium rates. A balance between both may be achieved in the right plan.
Additional protection
A term plan may offer you additional protection beyond the death benefit in the form of a rider. This may include accidental death and disability riders, critical illness coverage, or the waiver of premium rider. These riders may considerably enhance your term plan's coverage, at a nomnal extra cost. Hence, do check the riders offered under different term plans and how they may be relevant to your needs.
The claim settlement ratio of the insurer
This ratio refers to the number of claims settled by an insurer against the total number of claims received, within a particular year. A high claim settlement ratio may indicate the insurer has a good track record of settling claims. Therefore, you may want to review the claim settlement ratio of different insurers and choose one with a high claim settlement ratio.
Premium payment options
When you compare term insurance plans, you may come across premium payment options, such as limited or regular premium payments. You may also get to select the frequency at which you may want to pay the premiums, such as monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or yearly. Choosing the right option may help you manage your finances better and ensure your policy continues to be valid throughout its duration.
Limitations of the plans
When going through multiple plans for comparison, you may be looking at each one’s positive points. However, understanding the cons of each policy may be important, too. For instance, some term plans may not cover demise due to certain conditions or illnesses. If you are particularly vulnerable to such illness or condition, you may want to look for another type of term plan. Similarly, some plans may have a higher premium if you have a particular condition, while others may not. Hence, you may want to check these aspects thoroughly before you go ahead with the final decision.
Maximum policy duration offered
The duration of your term insurance policy may be such that your family receives financial security for a long time against risks to your life. Plans with shorter durations may mean a lower premium. However, they may not be adequate for you in the long run. Hence, do look for a sufficient policy duration when you carry out a comparison of term plans.
Compare Term Insurance Plans: Understanding the Different Types
Each policyholder may have their own unique needs. What one may want from their term plan may differ from what another may need. Hence, different types of term plans may exist, to suit these varying needs.
Let’s look at three popular types of term insurance plans you may come across in your term insurance comparison process:
Pure Term Insurance Plans
These kinds of term plans may be the most straightforward type of term insurance. They may provide a pre-fixed sum assured to the nominees in case of the policyholder’s untimely demise during the policy tenure. Usually, the premium for these policies may be relatively low, making them an affordable option if you seek a basic level of financial protection. Pure term plans do not have a maturity benefit component either. Hence, the policy may cease to provide any benefits once the policy term is completed.
TROP (Return of Premium) Plans
This label may be referred to for term insurance policies having a maturity component to them. A TROP plan may offer to return the total of all the premiums paid by the policyholder if they survive the policy term. This may mean that if the policyholder outlives the policy term, they may receive the total premiums paid as a lump sum. However, if they pass away during the term, the nominees only receive the death benefit amount.
Due to the return of premium feature, the premium for these plans may be higher when compared to basic term insurance plans.
Increasing and Decreasing Sum Assured Term Plans
For those individuals whose term plan needs may change over time, there are increasing and decreasing sum assured term plans. An increasing sum assured term plan is one where the sum assured may increase at fixed intervals by a certain percentage. This type of plan is ideal for individuals wanting their life cover to keep up with rising rates of inflation. On the other hand, in a decreasing sum assured term plan, the sum assured may decrease at certain intervals by a fixed percentage. Individuals who may have taken a loan or have liabilities decreasing over time may find a decreasing sum assured term plan suitable.
When you compare a term plan with another, ensure you do so with term plans of the same type. Putting a return of premium term plan against a basic term insurance policy may not be a fair comparison.
Compare Term Insurance Plans to Ensure Lifetime's Worth of Financial Security!
Some may buy term insurance plans to secure their family’s future. Hence, it may be immensely important to select the right policy after a thorough comparison of all the options available to you. This may help you and your loved ones receive the utmost financial security from the term plan.
Let’s look at some benefits the right term insurance plan may bring to you and your family:
Financial support during difficult times
Right now, you may be able to support your family through your income. However, an unfortunate event may occur at any time and take away the support you provide to your family. Managing expenses may become difficult. It may be during such situations that the right term plan may come to the rescue.
The financial support from the plan may help your family deal with the financial consequences of your absence so that they may continue to live their life with purpose. Your spouse may be able to manage daily expenses more easily and your child may be able to continue their education without financial worries.
Peace of mind
Securing your family’s future may be an important task. After all, if their future is secured the right way, you may have better peace of mind. You may rest better, knowing that if life were to throw a curveball at you, the repercussions of such an event may not affect your family financially.
Since this task may be of such significance, it may be better if it is carried out with due thought and consideration. Hence, it may help you to compare term insurance plans before finalising any plan.
Frequently Asked Questions
Can I have two term insurance plans at the same time?
If your term insurance coverage requirement may be high, then you may think of buying two term plans. They may provide you coverage simultaneously. If an unfortunate event were to occur, your family might benefit from the death benefit amounts from two plans. However, this may also mean that you pay two premiums.
So, you may want to think it over or consult a financial advisor before you decide whether you want two term plans. Ensure you disclose about the same to your insurers.
Is it possible to extend the existing term plan duration?
In most cases, you may not have the option to extend your term plan since most policies may not have a renewal feature. The coverage of a term plan may end once the duration ends.
Is the process to buy online term insurance plans safe?
Yes, you may buy an online term insurance plan without worries about safety or security. The payment portals of reputed insurance companies are safe and your private information, too, stays private.
What’s more, the validity of an online term plan may be as strong as that of an offline plan.
What is the main difference between a term plan and an endowment insurance plan?
A term plan and an endowment are both life insurance products. The main factor that distinguishes them is their coverage and other offerings. While term insurance plans can be regarded as pure protection plans with no savings or investment component, endowment plans also have a savings component attached to them.
What are the pay-out options in term life insurance?
Usually, you may come across two types of pay-outs when you compare term insurance plans:
Lump-sum death benefit - Here, the sum assured may be paid out in a lump-sum to the nominees immediately after the demise of the policyholder.
Lump-sum benefit with monthly pay-outs - Here, along with a certain percentage of the sum assured being paid out on the demise of the policyholder, a monthly pay-out may also be given to the nominees.
With some plans, you may also have the option of increasing monthly pay-outs wherein the monthly amount received by the nominees increases gradually.
How can I check how much coverage I need in a term insurance plan before I purchase it?
To get an idea of how much coverage you may require, you may want to use a term insurance calculator. This useful tool may ask you for certain details, such as your current income, the expected rise in income, and so on, to aid you in selecting the right sum insured.
However, do note that the figure provided by the term insurance calculator may only be an estimate. Do consult with a financial advisor before you finalise the sum assured of your term insurance plan.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. changes.word_original: In most cases, you may not have the option to extend your term plan since most policies may not have a renewal feature.
changes.pdf_final: You may not have the option to extend your term plan duration, once the policy is issued.

changes.word_original: The coverage of a term plan may end once the duration ends.
changes.pdf_final: However, it will depend on the policy terms and conditions. 2.

changes.word_original: Yes, you may buy an online term insurance plan without worries about safety or security.
changes.pdf_final: Yes, you may buy an online term insurance plan without worrying about safety or security.

changes.word_original: The payment portals of reputed insurance companies are safe and your private information, too, stays private.
changes.pdf_final: The payment portals are generally safe and your private information, too, stays private. 3.

changes.word_original: What’s more, the validity of an online term plan may be as strong as that of an offline plan.

changes.word_original: While term insurance plans can be regarded as pure protection plans with no savings or investment component, endowment plans also have a savings component attached to them.
changes.pdf_final: While term insurance plans can be regarded as pure protection plans with no savings or investment component, endowment plans also have a savings component attached to them. 4.

changes.word_original: Usually, you may come across two types of pay-outs when you compare term insurance plans:
changes.pdf_final: Usually, you may come across two types of pay-outs when you compare term insurance plans:  Lump-sum death benefit - Here, the sum assured may be paid out in a lump-sum to the nominees immediately after the demise of the life assured.  Lump-sum benefit with monthly pay-outs - Here, along with a certain percentage of the sum assured being paid out on the demise of the life assured, a monthly pay-out may also be given to the nominees.

changes.word_original: Lump-sum death benefit - Here, the sum assured may be paid out in a lump-sum to the nominees immediately after the demise of the policyholder.
changes.pdf_final: With some plans, you may also have the option of increasing monthly pay-outs wherein the monthly amount received by the nominees increases gradually. 5.

changes.word_original: Lump-sum benefit with monthly pay-outs - Here, along with a certain percentage of the sum assured being paid out on the demise of the policyholder, a monthly pay-out may also be given to the nominees.

changes.word_original: This useful tool may ask you for certain details, such as your current income, the expected rise in income, and so on, to aid you in selecting the right sum insured.
